The Climb · Zodi Karma

Zodi Karma is the merit your animal gathers: the daily visit, the proverbs studied, the moons checked, the matches tested. It buys nothing. It only lifts you, station by station, from Wanderer to Blessed.

  • ✦Blessed by the Gods of Zodi100,000

鳽 Sunbittern
Your Readingabout 6 min Meet the Animal The Crossing Your Element The Primal Mirror The Awakened Animal Your Lifeabout 5 min Working Nature Ideal Habitat Growth Go Deeperbrowse Moon Rhythm Your Proverb Your Peopleplay Relationships Related Animals The Bond Test

0 of 13 chapters read

♌ Leo and 狗 Year of the Dog

Sunbittern

You stay the color of the riverbank until something forces your wings open into twin suns.

Zodi Animal · No. 059 / 144

In brief

Your Primal Zodiac Animal is the Sunbittern: Leo's need to be seen crossed with the Dog's patient watch over one piece of ground.

  • Leo

    gives you a brightness built to be the center of the room, and the nerve to actually stand in it.

  • The Dog

    gives you the loyalty to hold one stretch of ground and the patience to wait there without being asked.

  • The Sunbittern

    turns both into one instinct: disappear into the bank, and open into the center of the room only when it counts.

One strength, one cost: the same stillness that lets your brightness land at exactly the right moment can also mean it never gets used at all. This reading traces where that instinct comes from, and what it becomes when it grows up.

Meet the Sunbittern

A plain brown wader on a plain brown bank, until the wings open and two eyes stare back.

A solitary wader of Central and South America's forest streams, barred in brown and grey until the instant it is not.

Everything this reading says about you is grounded in what the Sunbittern actually is: a bird built to be missed at a glance, a threat display that turns its whole body into a pair of enormous staring eyes, and a lineage so old its only living relative stands on the other side of the planet. This chapter is that foundation. The rest of the page draws its meaning from these real traits.

The eyes were never the problem. Knowing when to open them was.

6 traits below

01of six

The plain approach

Animal fact

At rest the Sunbittern is a close, fine barring of black, brown, and grey, and the bird is often overlooked even where it is common, standing quietly along a stream or swamp edge.

Our reading

You are built to read as ordinary at a glance, and most rooms take you at that valuation. It costs you nothing, until the room never learns there was anything else to see.

02of six

The sunburst

Animal fact

When alarmed, the Sunbittern spreads both wings and its tail into a wide, flat display of chestnut and gold false eyespots, a pattern invisible when the bird is folded and impossible to miss when it is not.

Our reading

What you are holding does not grow slowly into view. It arrives at full size, all at once, and the people who only knew the folded version are rarely prepared for the rest of you.

03of six

The strike

Animal fact

The Sunbittern hunts by walking a stream edge in slow, deliberate steps and striking suddenly with its long neck and spear-shaped bill to take fish, amphibians, crustaceans, and insects.

Our reading

Your patience is not the opposite of your speed. It is the thing that makes the speed exact. You watch a long time so that when you finally move, you only have to move once.

04of six

One bird, one bank

Animal fact

Sunbitterns are typically found singly or in pairs, holding a stretch of stream or forest water rather than gathering in flocks.

Our reading

You do not need a crowd to feel accompanied. One stretch of ground and, sometimes, one other creature on it is the whole of what you require.

05of six

The false injury

Animal fact

If a nest is approached, a parent Sunbittern will often perform a broken-wing display, feigning injury to draw a predator away from the chicks before flying off unharmed.

Our reading

You will make yourself the visible, vulnerable thing on purpose, if it moves the danger away from whoever is behind you. The performance is a choice, not a failure of nerve.

06of six

The lone lineage

Animal fact

The Sunbittern is the sole member of its family, Eurypygidae, and genetic study places its nearest living relative as the kagu, a flightless bird found only in New Caledonia, on the far side of the Pacific.

Our reading

You do not come from a crowd of similar animals. Whatever you actually are, you have mostly been working it out alone.

The Sunbittern dossier

The Dossier

Zodi Animal
No. 059 / 144

☼ Sunbittern · Leo × Dog

The Folded Sunburst

Stands like part of the bank, strikes like it never moved, and opens into something the bank never warned you about.

Profile

Archetype
The Folded Sunburst
Central gift
Timing the open exactly right
Central shadow
Holding the display past its moment
Protective instinct
Go still, then go enormous
Growth lesson
Open before you are cornered
Power phase
Full Moon
Element
Earth
Ideal habitat
One still bank, one open floor

These five traits are yours too. Take the test to see whether you hold the display the way the Sunbittern does, folded until the room actually deserves it.

Take the personality test

Tap a trait to see it in yourself

    SunbitternPick a trait above to see how strongly it tends to show up in you, and which way it leans.

    Two zodiacs, one animal

    Where Leo and the Dog meet

    Leo brings

    • A brightness built to be the center of the room, not an accident of good lighting
    • The confidence to make an entrance feel intentional, even after minutes of standing still
    • A standard for how the moment deserves to be met, once it actually arrives

    The Dog brings

    • Loyalty to one stretch of ground, held quietly and without complaint
    • A watchfulness that reads danger and safety before either has fully arrived
    • A preference for the earned display over the constant one

    What the crossing makes

    Cross them and you get a creature that can fill a room and mostly chooses not to. The brightness is real, held in reserve rather than withheld out of fear, and the watch is real too, patient rather than anxious. Neither half performs alone: the Dog keeps Leo from spending the display on people who were never going to deserve it, and Leo keeps the Dog from mistaking a quiet stretch of ground for the whole of a life.

    Where they pull against each other

    Leo wants to be seen; the Dog wants to hold its post and say nothing until it matters. When the watch runs long enough, waiting can start to look identical to hiding, and only you know which one it actually is. The chapters ahead are largely about telling the two apart before the room stops checking back.

    Drag the dial to weigh your two halves and watch the blend change

    Leo
    Dog
    50 Leo In balance Dog 50

    In balance, you hold the bank, read what actually needs you, and open completely when it does. The display costs nothing because it is never wasted, and nobody who has seen it forgets it.

    Your element

    土

    Your element is Earth

    Earth's archetype: The Nurturer · in the Sunbittern: The Silted Bank

    Earth's archetype in this system is the Nurturer. In the Sunbittern it settles into something more specific: the Silted Bank, ground that looks like nothing worth a second look until the current shifts and shows what color it was holding underneath. Earth at its best is the ground that holds its shape and quietly feeds what stands on it: patient, fertile, in no hurry to be admired. In excess it does not just hold, it flattens, going so still that even the thing worth showing gets buried under the same brown quiet as everything else. The Shadow chapter ahead wears this same signature.

    How this was calculated

    Every Zodi Animal takes its element from the fixed element of its Chinese year-animal, and for the Dog that is Earth. Your Western sign adds a second note: Leo carries Fire, and in the five-phase cycle Fire feeds Earth, ash becoming new ground. So the two halves sit in sequence rather than in conflict: Leo's fire is the heat, and the Dog's earth is what receives it and holds its shape long after the flame has passed through. The pairing of animal and element archetype is Zodi Animal's own reading of these traditional systems, offered for reflection.

    DirectionCenter
    SeasonLate summer / between seasons
    VirtueFidelity
    ColorYellow

    The elements around you

    Wu Xing 五行, the Five Phases, is a system of relationships, not isolated traits. Here is how the other four move around yours.

    Generates you

    火Fire feeds you, the way the sun bakes a bank hard by noon

    In the generating cycle, Fire turns to ash and the ash becomes ground. For you, that fire is your own Leo half, the flare of confidence that, once it has burned through, settles into the quiet, held stillness the Dog's Earth is known for. Time spent near real heat, an audience, a risk, a stage, feeds a steadiness that outlasts the moment itself.

    Balances you

    木Wood checks you, the way a root finds the seam in dried mud

    In the controlling cycle, Wood splits Earth open from underneath. For you, Wood is the honest question, the person who will not accept the folded, barred version as the whole answer. A little of that disruption is what keeps your stillness a choice instead of a permanent address.

    You generate

    金You feed Metal, the way pressure turns silt into stone

    In the generating cycle, Earth compresses into Metal over time, ground becoming ore. What you hold still long enough hardens into precision: the exact second to strike, the exact moment to open, the exact word you have been holding since the room went quiet.

    You restrain

    水You restrain Water, the way a bank gives a stream its course

    In the controlling cycle, Earth channels Water rather than stopping it. For you, this is the steadiness you bring to a room that is flooding with feeling: not damming it, just giving it banks it can be found inside, the way the stream you stand beside every day still remembers exactly where it is allowed to run.

    Go deeper: Bazi 八字

    This is Wu Xing 五行, and it runs deeper

    Your Earth comes from one place: the Dog, whose fixed element anchors the whole Sunbittern. But Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal, and Water are not fixed labels. They are a grammar for how energy feeds and wears on itself, and the Dog's Earth is only your starting note.

    Bazi 八字, the Four Pillars, reads that grammar across your whole chart: year, month, day, and the exact hour you were born. The Sunbittern takes the element of just one pillar, your Dog year. Your full chart also carries the Fire that Leo lends, in proportions only your birth time can show.

    BaziRead your Four Pillars ElementsSee all five phases

    Go deeper: Chakras

    Earth in the body

    Held beside the yogic map, Earth finds its closest match in the Root chakra, Muladhara: the base that governs safety and the sense of solid ground underfoot, an apt image for a bird that reads as part of the ground until it decides otherwise. Its bija sound is LAM; its element there is earth.

    Treat the pairing as an interpretive bridge between two traditions, not an equivalence.

    Root chakraMuladharaBija: LAMElement: earth
    ChakraVisit your Root chakra PracticeYoga for your element

    Take it further

    土ElementThe Earth element in full OverviewAll five elements Bazi · 八字Your Four Pillars Feng shuiSet up your workspace

    The Primal Mirror

    Every reading passes five gates, in order

    One mechanism, five states. Reflex, capacity, defense, distortion, integration.

    Swipe the gates · tap to open each one

    01 · Reflex

    You go still before you decide anything

    The room shifts, and you have already stopped moving before you have named why.

    Before thought gets a vote, your body has already chosen the bank. A voice changes register, a stranger's attention lands somewhere near you, and you go quiet and barred, blending into whatever is already in the room. It is not fear exactly. It is the oldest, most reliable strategy you own, and it has never once failed to buy you time.

    The brightness does not vanish while you hold still. It waits, fully intact, folded along your spine, for a decision you have not consciously made yet about whether this room has earned it.

    02 · Capacity

    You open completely, and only when it counts

    When you finally show the whole of it, nobody in the room mistakes it for anything less than real.

    That same stillness, given room to work, becomes a rare kind of credibility. You do not perform warmth to fill a silence, so when the warmth shows up, people register it as true rather than habitual. The watching is not withholding. It is quality control on something that actually deserves the room's attention.

    You also strike cleanly, because you spent the waiting time reading the whole situation instead of half-committing to it early. What you decide, once you decide it, tends to be exactly right.

    03 · Defense

    The stillness is protecting the display

    You keep the bright thing folded so it only ever gets shown to a bank that has earned it.

    What the gift is guarding is the display itself, the brightness, the warmth, the unguarded version of you that shows up fully or not at all. Somewhere in your history, opening too early or too often taught you that a display without an audience worth it just gets treated as ordinary, or worse, ignored entirely.

    So you learned to fold it and hold your ground, extending the same instinct to the people you love: watch the room first, protect the bright thing, open it only where it is safe.

    04 · Distortion

    Shadow: the display that stays folded too long

    A stillness this reliable can always find one more reason the room is not the right one yet.

    What trips it. Being asked to open before you have finished reading the room, or a room that expects the display as a default rather than as something earned.

    What your mind says. Not yet. This isn't the bank for it.

    What you do. You hold the pose a little longer. Then longer again. The wings stay folded for one more season, and you call it discernment.

    What it costs. The people who would have gone quiet at the sight of you never get the chance, because from ten paces you read as nothing worth a second look. <b>A sunburst that never opens is not being saved. It is being mistaken for a plain brown bird.</b>

    In love

    You give a partner your loyalty and your watchfulness and hold the actual warmth in reserve, then wonder why they say they cannot tell how you feel.

    At work

    You clock the right call in the first five minutes and wait for someone to ask you directly, which they rarely think to do.

    With friends

    You are the steady, dependable one at the edge of every gathering, and the last one anyone thinks to check on.

    05 · Integration

    Awakened: the same strength, integrated

    You still go still. You still read the room before you commit to it. You just stop needing the room to be perfect before you open.

    The awakened Sunbittern keeps the whole mechanism: the fold, the wait, the sudden full display. What changes is the threshold. Good enough replaces perfect, because perfect was never a real bank, it was a way of never actually stepping into the current. You spend the stillness deciding which room deserves you, not proving that none of them do.

    You open before you are cornered into it, in ordinary light, and the room turns out to have been waiting longer than you assumed.

    Name the form you are becoming

    Complete the sentence: I open when I

    Beyond the reading · ✦ Purple Star

    Meet the awakened Sunbittern

    The Mirror showed you the awakened form in outline. Purple Star astrology, Zi Wei Dou Shu (紫微斗数), is the oldest and most detailed of the Chinese chart systems, and it maps the Sunbittern you are becoming, not only the one you are.

    Keep reading it, in your dashboard

    A free account saves your chart and brings a new reading for your animal each week.

    紫微

    Your full Purple Star chart

    All fourteen stars across your twelve palaces, cast for your Leo Dog crossing and saved to your dashboard.

     ☼

    The awakened Sunbittern, tracked

    Your path from the fallen form to the awakened one, followed over time, with a fresh reading for your animal every week.
    縁

    The karmic fortune of five

    Invite five friends to find their Zodi Animal, and your Zodi Karma opens the Circle reading: how the six of you move together.

    Create your free account

    Free. No card. Your chart, your weekly reading, and your invited circle, kept in one place.

    Career and working life

    Your working nature

    An application of the archetype, not a verdict: you are likely to work best where your judgment is trusted quietly, and you are not asked to perform warmth on a schedule that has nothing to do with what the room actually needs.

    01 / 05

    You lead by being right after everyone stopped watching

    You lead through timing rather than volume. You hold position while a plan runs its early, messy course, then strike once, precisely, at the moment the room can actually use the correction. The people who have learned to wait for that moment get an answer worth more than an hour of earlier noise.

    The failure mode is structural. A correction that only ever arrives once the meeting has ended never gets to change the meeting, and a team eventually stops crediting the good call to the person who made it two minutes too late to matter.

    02 / 05

    One clear bank, and permission to wait

    Time to read the situation before you are asked to commit to it. Work judged on the strike itself, not on how quickly you announced you were about to make one. A small circle who already know the display is real when it finally shows up.

    Environments that drain this animal: constant forced visibility, open-plan pressure to perform enthusiasm on command, cultures that read stillness as absence rather than as the actual work.

    • Time to read the room
    • Judged on the strike
    • A circle that already trusts you
    • Permission to stay barred

    03 / 05

    Slow to commit, exact once you do

    Your decision pattern is the Dog's: hold the ground, watch fully, move once. It serves you when the stakes reward precision and costs you when the window is short. Set a deadline for the watching, not for the decision, and make it earlier than feels comfortable.

    Your delegation pattern folds the same way: you hand out the visible tasks and keep your own read of the situation to yourself unless directly asked. Practice volunteering the read before anyone has to ask for it, once a quarter, and notice the room does not collapse.

    04 / 05

    Two ways the display stays folded

    The first: you are asked to open before you have finished reading the room, and the whole animal locks. You give a barred, careful, technically correct answer while the real read sits behind your teeth.

    The second is quieter. You wait so long to open that waiting becomes the whole identity, and then it is not caution anymore, it is habit, and habit is much harder to notice, let alone argue with. The burnout pattern here is not collapse. It is a long, competent silence, delivered by someone who was right the entire time and said so to almost no one.

    05 / 05

    A reputation, struck once and remembered

    Your edge is timing, and timing only compounds when someone actually saw the strike land. The long game is building a record of visible, well-placed calls made in rooms that were merely good enough, not perfect.

    One growth practice: each month, open the display before the room forces it out of you. Track what it actually cost. It will be smaller than the watching predicted.

    Your Ideal Habitat

    Living with your Zodi Animal

    One still bank, one floor to open on.

    The Sunbittern holds a stretch of stream, walks it slowly, and needs one open patch of ground for the display to actually read when it happens. Translated to a room: a defensible, familiar base, and one place in the home built for nothing but being fully seen. Every recommendation below follows from that: earth tones to disappear into, one warm accent because the display has to have somewhere to go, and never a seat with its back to a room you have not already read.

    Your feng shui palette

    Feng shui builds a room from your element and the one that feeds it. Earth is yours, the ground you disappear into; Fire is what feeds it, the warmth that gives the ground something worth holding onto. The bar shows roughly how much of each to use, so you can build a bank to blend into and a floor to open on.

    Most of the roomA small accent

    GroundEarthAbout 45% of the room

    Riverbank umber

    Your own Earth tone, the color of wet clay and last season's leaves. The bank you disappear into: walls, floors, the large quiet surfaces.

    SupportEarthAbout 29% of the room

    Dry reed gold

    Earth's lighter tone, the color of the barred feathers at rest. Textiles, furniture, anything within easy reach.

    AccentFireAbout 17% of the room

    Sunburst chestnut

    Fire feeds Earth, so this warm chestnut-red keeps your ground from going flat and cold. It is also the exact color the wings show when they finally open. Cushions, art, smaller pieces.

    SparkFireAbout 9% of the room

    Eyespot gold

    Concentrated Fire, the gold at the center of the display. One or two small touches, so the room has somewhere for the brightness to go when it is finally shown.

    Arrange your whole space with feng shui

    Workspace

    A desk positioned so your back is covered and the door is in clear view. This animal cannot settle into real work while any part of it is still reading the entrance.

    Bedroom

    Kept dim, warm-toned, and enclosed, the one room with no expectation of the display at all. You recover by folding completely, not by performing rest for anyone watching.

    Entry

    One genuinely open, well-lit threshold, where arrival is brief and does not require the watch to stay up the whole time. The house should have one place where being seen costs nothing.

    Living area

    A seat with a full view of the room, and a small, warm circle rather than an open floor built for a crowd you will spend the evening reading instead of enjoying.

    The open floor

    One bright, uncluttered patch of the home, kept clear on purpose, held for the rare, chosen moments the whole of you is meant to be seen. It does not need to be large. It needs to exist.

    Reflection

    A low, warm corner near something the color of the bank, for the full-moon practice of naming what has actually earned the display this cycle.

    Elements to avoid

    • A seat with its back to the room; the reading never stops from there
    • A home with no bright, open patch anywhere; the display needs somewhere to actually land
    • Cold, ungrounded rooms with nothing warm to rest against, which flatten the ground you need to disappear into
    • A layout that puts you on constant display with no bank to retreat to between rooms
    The five-minute reset

    Five-minute habitat reset: go still in a low, warm-toned corner, name one thing that is actually safe right now, and then walk once, slowly, into the brightest room in the home before returning to whatever you were doing.

    土 Feng Shui
    for the
    Sunbittern

    Free with a Zodi Animal account

    Redesign your space with feng shui

    A room-by-room guide tuned to your Earth element: arrange your home so it works with your nature instead of against it. Yours free when you make a Zodi Animal account.

    • Room by room, tuned to your Earth element
    • What to place, what to clear, and where
    • Free the moment you make an account
    Get the free eBook

    Feng shui elements here are symbolic. They support intention and act as visual reminders, not claims that any object, color, or direction produces wealth, health, romance, or success.

    Integration

    Practices for the Folded Sunburst

    Small, physical, repeatable. One from each room of your life.

    • The lesson

      The fold was built to make the display mean something, not to keep it hidden forever. The work is not to stop watching. It is to notice, honestly, when the bank has already become safe enough, and open before the room makes you.

    • A ritual

      At each full moon, when the light is at its fullest, name out loud the one place you will let yourself be fully seen this cycle, and the condition you are dropping in order to do it.

    • In your space

      Keep the one bright, open patch of your home actually clear, not storage, not a pile, so the display has somewhere ready to land the day you decide to give it.

    • In one bond

      This week, let one person see the display before they have to ask for it. Small is fine. Unprompted is the point.

    • At work

      Say the accurate read out loud in the meeting, not in the hallway after. Once. Then notice what it actually cost.

    Moon rhythm

    Your power moon

    The full moon is the month's most complete light, the sky doing all at once what the Sunbittern does only when it decides to. It fits the crossing that spends most of its life folded: this is the one window built for full, deliberate display. What to do: in full light, where everyone can actually see you, open the thing you have been holding. What to avoid: mistaking the brightness of the night for permission to perform on a bank that has not actually earned it.

    Your next full moon · Power Moon

    Wednesday, July 29, 2026

    19 days from today.

    When it arrives, stand somewhere genuinely well lit and open the thing you have been folding all month. The stillness was never the problem for you. Keeping the wings shut on a night built for the opposite is.

    1. Tue Jul 28The last held stillness. Name one thing you have kept folded that has already earned the open.
    2. Wed Jul 29 · Full MoonThe open. Choose one room, one person, and show the whole of it without waiting to be asked.
    3. Thu Jul 30The fold returns. Notice that opening once did not cost you the stillness you rely on.
    Explore the Moon hub

    The proverb of your year

    犬quǎn馬mǎ之zhī勞láo

    Tap a tile to hear one character

    Literally the toil of a dog or a horse

    Meaning A modest offer of devoted service, given without expecting to be thanked or even noticed.

    The dog and the horse in the old phrase do not perform their loyalty. They simply keep working at it, unannounced, until it is needed. You keep the same kind of watch: folded, ordinary, present on the bank long before anyone asked you to be. But a devotion this quiet can be mistaken for absence if it never once shows what it has been guarding. The toil was never the problem. Letting it go unseen is.

    A proverb we tie to the Year of the Dog. The pairing is our own reading, not tradition.

    In relationship

    How the Sunbittern loves

    You will hold your ground for someone for years before you let them see what you actually look like at full display.

    Care, for you, is watchfulness: reading what a person needs before they ask, holding your post near them without being told to. The pattern underneath decides how open you let any bond get. Being fully seen is the thing you want most and the thing you postpone longest.

    How you show love

    By staying close and reading correctly. You notice a change in someone before they have named it themselves, and you adjust your whole footing around it without announcing that you did.

    What makes you feel safe

    A steady presence that does not demand the display on a schedule, and does not treat the folded, ordinary version of you as the whole story.

    What they may misunderstand

    Your stillness can read as distance or disinterest. It is neither. It is how you decide the ground is safe enough to open on.

    Your conflict pattern

    You go barred and quiet, holding your position at a slight distance until you have finished reading what actually happened.

    What to practice

    Open the display before the disagreement forces it out of you. Say the warm true thing while it is still optional.

    How you show up

    As the one who is already there, holding the same stretch of ground, reliable in a way that rarely gets mentioned out loud.

    What they may misunderstand

    Because you rarely perform the friendship, people can assume it costs you nothing to keep it. It costs you the same as it does anyone; you simply do not display the cost.

    What to practice

    Let one friend see the display without a reason attached to it. No occasion. Just the whole of you, once, unprompted.

    How you show up

    You read the temperature of the house before you are fully through the door, and hold your position accordingly.

    What they may misunderstand

    They mistake your steadiness for having nothing going on underneath it. You are simply the one who does not announce what is going on.

    What to practice

    Let the household see one unguarded moment without immediately folding back into the barred, careful version.

    How you show up

    The quiet, accurate one at the edge of the room, correct long before anyone asks your opinion directly.

    Your conflict pattern

    You fold the display rather than defend it, letting a louder, worse idea win a room you had already read.

    What to practice

    Open in the meeting. The bank after the meeting has already stopped listening.

    Healthy support, for this animal, does not chase the display and does not accept the folded version as final. It waits near your bank without crowding it, notices when the ground has actually become safe, and stays close enough to see you open when you finally do. Choose that over anyone who only ever meets the barred, careful version and calls it the whole of you.

    Test this bond with a second birth date

    Computed across all 143 other crossings

    Matches the wheel favors

    Strongest pull

    Match I · of three

    火

    Pronghorn

    ♈ Aries × 马 Horse

    1942, 1954, 1966, 1978, 1990, 2002, 2014

    Same trineTheir Fire feeds your Earth

    The Horse shares the Dog's trine, so effort between you compounds. Their Fire feeds your Earth: this one is fuel.

    Read the Pronghorn

    Match II · of three

    木

    Fennec Fox

    ♊ Gemini × 兔 Rabbit

    1951, 1963, 1975, 1987, 1999, 2011, 2023

    Secret friendsWood nature

    The Rabbit is the Dog's secret friend, the wheel's quiet allied pair.

    Read the Fennec Fox

    Match III · of three

    土

    Duetting Frog

    ♎ Libra × 狗 Dog

    1946, 1958, 1970, 1982, 1994, 2006, 2018

    Same yearShared Earth

    Two Dog years run on the same clock. Two Earth natures recognize each other on sight.

    Read the Duetting Frog

    Step onto the bank

    Where to go from the Sunbittern

    Three matches surfaced above; the wheel holds 140 more. Each crossing opens onto others, and these are meanings to explore, not verdicts. Contrast is a mirror for self-knowledge, never a warning.

    Best next reading

    See how Leo changes across the other eleven years

    You have watched Leo fuse with the Dog. The fastest way to feel the system's logic is to watch the same brightness land in eleven other bodies, most of which have no interest in folding it away first.

    All Leo animals

    Animals your instincts trust on sight

    Watchers who hold a bank and a bright thing folded away: kinship, not similarity.

    Golden PheasantLeo · Year of the Rabbit Moon BearCancer · Year of the Tiger Coconut CrabCancer · Year of the Rat

    The temperaments that stretch yours

    Full-time brightness, worn without a fold: your pattern, inverted.

    Humpback WhaleCancer · Year of the Dragon Sea OtterCancer · Year of the Rabbit

    Same year, other skies

    The Dog's gift under eleven other suns.

    Giant OtterAries · Year of the Dog Banded MongooseTaurus · Year of the Dog African Grey ParrotGemini · Year of the Dog Ribbon SealCancer · Year of the Dog

    Same sky, other years

    Leo's gift wearing eleven other bodies.

    The last chapter

    The Sunbittern is yours.
    Now find out who is worth opening for.

    The Bond Test

    Bring a friend into the reading

    One birthdate is all it takes. Turn the wheel to their year, and their animal steps out to meet the Sunbittern.

    Your first friend

    01 The year they were born
    牛 1995 · Year of the Pig
    02 The month
    03 The day

    The Moon tonightCome back at the full moon, your power phase, and open on purpose. Wander the MenagerieAll 144 crossings. Each one opens onto others.

    The Folded Sunburst · Leo × Dog · Earth 土

    Your line to keep

    I am the Sunbittern: I hold the bank until it's safe, then I open into something the bank never warned you about.

    鳽

    Where you go next

    Three doors are open. Pick one.

    You know the shape of the Sunbittern now. Here is what the reading did not tell you yet, and where each thread runs.

    Bonds

    Test a friend against your wheel

    Drop any name onto the Sunbittern wheel and see where they sit. Ally, mirror, or friction.

    Fire is what feeds this nature; Wood is what keeps it from staying folded forever.

    Run the Bond Test     Purple Star 紫微

    Your chart is bigger than one animal

    Purple Star astrology, Zi Wei Dou Shu, maps your whole birth chart: fourteen stars across twelve palaces, cast from your exact birth hour.

    The reading here is the outline your Leo Dog shares. Your own chart shows which palace runs loudest.

    Cast your chart

    Save your Sunbittern before you go.

    A free account keeps this reading and every animal you test, on any device. Come back to where you left off instead of a blank page.

    About a minute to set up. No card. Delete it anytime.

    Sends it to a friend.

    Sunbittern · 059 / 144 · Earth 土

    Pass it on

    Share your crossing

    Keep every reading you are given.

    Make it permanent

    This device will remember the Sunbittern until it doesn't. A free account keeps your animal, your reflections, and every reading you are given, renewed with each new moon, on any device.

    • Your Sunbittern, saved for good
    • Your identity line and keeper stone, kept
    • The feng shui eBook for your element, free
    Create your free account

    The Sunbittern Test

    Do you hold the display the way the Sunbittern does?

    The Sunbittern is built from five traits. Ten quick questions measure yours against its shape and tell you whether you are a true Sunbittern, or a rare one who carries them differently.

    5 traits 10 questions ≈ 2 minutes No account needed

      Sunbittern You

      How this reading is built

      Each Zodi Animal reading combines four threads: Western zodiac symbolism (your Sun sign), Chinese zodiac symbolism (your year animal), five-element interpretation (Wu Xing, via the year-animal's fixed element), and the real biology and behavior of the animal itself. The archetype that binds them, here the Folded Sunburst, is Zodi Animal's own framework. Readings are for reflection and self-exploration; they are not clinical assessment, scientific personality testing, or a claim about fate. Animal facts are drawn from zoological references and flagged in the text as Animal fact; interpretive material is flagged as Our reading.

      • IUCN Red List · Eurypyga helias
      • eBird · Sunbittern
      • Smithsonian's National Zoo · Sunbittern
      • Cytotaxonomy of Eurypyga helias (Gruiformes, Eurypygidae), PMC

      Last reviewed 2026-07-09

      Zodi Animal

      One hundred and forty-four animals, born where the Western Sun signs cross the Chinese zodiac years. A slow tradition of meaning, kept under the moon.

      The Daily Reading

      Your reading, at dawn.

      One short passage for your Zodi Animal each morning. The moon's phase, what it favors, and a line meant for you. Free, and it takes ten seconds to join.

      No noise. Unsubscribe anytime.
